name: Ornish's Pizza Dough for Bread Machines
recipeInstructions:
1. Set your bread machine to the "white bread" cycle and "manual," using the "turbo" setting if available.
2. Place the yeast, flour, sugar, salt, and warm water into the bread machine.
3. Let the dough run through the manual cycle without baking it. It should beep when finished.
4. Preheat your oven to 450°F (232°C).
5. Punch down the dough and transfer it from the machine onto a floured surface. Knead for about one minute.
6. Stretch the dough into a circle, ensuring it is flat in the middle and thicker around the edges.
7. Sprinkle cornmeal over a pizza stone or heavy baking pan.
8. Place the dough on the prepared stone or pan.
9. Add your desired toppings and herbs (such as oregano) to the pizza.
10. Bake for 10-15 minutes, until the crust is browned.
11. This recipe makes one 12-inch medium-thick-crust pizza.
Ideas for pizza toppings:
Sauce: canned tomatoes, drained, then pureed; or roasted red peppers, pureed (instead of tomatoes)
Herbs: basil, oregano, thyme, pepper, rosemary
(Wild) mushrooms, thinly sliced
Zucchini, thinly sliced, sauteed
Fresh chopped spinach and fresh sliced tomatoes
Nutritional yeast for a "cheesy" taste
